items.headline: UAE to Withdraw from OPEC, Effective May 1
items.body: The United Arab Emirates officially withdrew from OPEC, effective May 1, 2026. This action could reshape global energy dynamics and influence oil prices by increasing UAE output.

items.headline: Supreme Court Ruling Weakens Voting Rights Act Protections
items.body: The US Supreme Court struck down a Louisiana congressional map, citing racial gerrymandering. This decision makes proving future Voting Rights Act violations significantly harder for minority districts.

items.headline: Overnight Policy Change Disrupts Lives of 500,000 US Workers
items.body: A sudden US policy change terminated contracts and suspended work permits for hundreds of thousands. This disruption creates financial strain for families and could cause broader economic ripple effects.

items.headline: Russia Continues Deportation and Indoctrination of Ukrainian Children
items.body: Russian officials are deporting children from occupied Mariupol to St. Petersburg for cultural indoctrination. This forced re-education profoundly impacts Ukrainian identity and future generations.

items.headline: Japan-flagged oil tanker crosses Strait of Hormuz for first time since Iran war began
items.body: The Idemitsu Maru became the first Japanese-flagged tanker to transit the strait since conflict started February 28. Japan confirmed no fees were paid to Iran for passage.

items.headline: Bulgaria opposition coalition wins outright parliamentary majority ending years of political crisis
items.body: Progressive Bulgaria secured 130 seats with 44.7 percent of votes counted in parliamentary elections. No Bulgarian party has won an outright majority this century.

items.headline: Highway bomb attack kills 19 ahead of Colombia presidential election next month
items.body: An explosive struck a bus on the Pan-American Highway in Cauca department on April 25. Military officials recorded 26 attacks across two southwestern departments over two days.

items.headline: China detains nearly 70 Panamanian-flagged ships after Supreme Court canal ruling
items.body: Beijing detained the vessels in March following a court decision on Panama Canal operations. Six countries including the United States issued a joint statement condemning the economic retaliation.
